sanskrit: द्यावापृथिव्योरिदमन्तरं हि व्याप्तं त्वयैकेन दिशश्च सर्वाः। दृष्ट्वाद्भुतं रूपमुग्रं तवेदं लोकत्रयं प्रव्यथितं महात्मन्॥
transliteration: dyāv ā-pṛthivyor idam antaraṃ hi vyāptaṃ tvayaikena diśaś ca sarvāḥ | dṛṣṭvādbhutaṃ rūpam ugraṃ tavedaṃ loka-trayaṃ pravyathitaṃ mahātman ||
literal_meaning: Between heaven and earth, indeed all this space is pervaded by You alone; all directions are filled. Seeing this wondrous, terrible form of Yours, the three worlds are trembling, O Great Soul.
contextual_meaning: Arjuna’s ego-boundaries dissolve as he confronts the archetype of the Terrible Mother/Father—an omnipresent, overwhelming reality that simultaneously contains and threatens him. The psyche registers awe, dread, and a loss of control.
psychological_state: Acute dissociative awe bordering on depersonalization; the self–other boundary collapses under the pressure of transpersonal magnitude.
trigger_scenario: A high-functioning CEO watches satellite footage of a mega-hurricane covering the entire hemisphere and realizes the company, the city, and personal identity are microscopic within the storm’s eye.
anchor_text: Feel the awe that dissolves your small self into boundless being.
